name: Theaster Gates
practice: Art as urban infrastructure, abandoned building activation, Black material culture
approach: Buy abandoned buildings on the South Side of Chicago for $1. Transform them into libraries, galleries, listening rooms, archives. Art practice IS real estate practice IS community development. The building is the medium. The neighborhood is the canvas. Culture is infrastructure.
body_of_work: Dorchester Projects — abandoned houses became Archive House, Listening House, Black Cinema House. Stony Island Arts Bank — abandoned bank became cultural center. Rebuild Foundation. His work has increased property values and community engagement across Grand Crossing and Washington Park.
why_instructive: He IS a sphere producer in practice. Every project activates a physical space (sphere layer 7), creates permanence (layer 8), changes policy perception (layer 9), and catalyzes adjacent activation (layer 10). He proves the sphere schema works.

dome_relevance.5: Housing — abandoned buildings as housing for culture
dome_relevance.8: Community — art practice as community building
dome_relevance.11: Creativity — the building itself as artistic medium

sphere_relevance.1: Parcel — buying abandoned parcels, changing ownership and use
sphere_relevance.4: Economic — $1 purchases becoming multi-million dollar cultural assets
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — honoring building history while creating new use
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — every building becomes an activated cultural space
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — institutions that outlast the initial intervention
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — changed how Chicago thinks about vacant property
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — each project catalyzes the next block
